summary.executive_summary: The QC analysis for request 50004-000001 identifies a critical systematic failure in MSH2 coverage alongside highly inconsistent enrichment metrics across all samples. Mean coverage depth frequently drops to 20x, and on-target mapping rates are unacceptably low, reaching a floor of 10%. Immediate remediation of the panel design and capture protocol is required to ensure clinical sensitivity and diagnostic integrity.

findings.title: Systematic MSH2 Exon 8 Coverage Dropout
findings.detail: Recurrent low coverage at Chr2:47442251 (MSH2 exon 8) in 7 out of 8 samples confirms a systematic panel design flaw rather than stochastic sample failure. This locus is critical for Lynch syndrome screening, and current depth is insufficient for reliable variant calling.
findings.action: Initiate immediate probe redesign for the MSH2 locus and implement mandatory Sanger sequencing backfill for this region for all Lynch syndrome referrals.

findings.title: Sub-threshold Mean Coverage Depth
findings.detail: Multiple samples exhibit a mean coverage depth of 20x, which falls significantly below the clinical threshold required for high-confidence heterozygous variant detection. This is exacerbated by a high count of low coverage regions (up to 150), compromising the analytical sensitivity of the assay.
findings.action: Increase the total sequencing output per sample or optimize library preparation to ensure a minimum mean coverage depth of 100x across the target territory.

findings.title: Inconsistent and Low On-target Mapping Rate
findings.detail: The on-target mapping rate is highly variable and poor, ranging from 10% to 50%, indicating significant off-target capture and inefficient library enrichment. This suggests suboptimal hybridization kinetics or non-specific binding during the capture step.
findings.action: Re-evaluate and optimize the hybridization and wash stringency parameters to improve capture specificity and increase the on-target mapping rate.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: Something interesting came out of this week's sequencing run for request 50004-000001, but unfortunately, it is not the kind of discovery we want to see in a clinical pipeline.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: We have identified a systematic failure in MSH2 coverage, specifically a recurrent dropout at exon 8. This is not a stochastic sample failure; it is a clear panel design flaw.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: This locus at Chromosome 2, position 47442251, is critical for Lynch syndrome screening. Currently, the depth is insufficient for reliable variant calling, which directly compromises our diagnostic integrity.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: The enrichment metrics are highly inconsistent. We are seeing mean coverage depths plummeting to 20x, which is far below the clinical threshold required for high-confidence heterozygous variant detection.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: Furthermore, on-target mapping rates are reaching a floor of 10%. This indicates significant off-target capture and suboptimal hybridization kinetics during the enrichment step.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: With up to 150 low-coverage regions per sample, the analytical sensitivity of the assay is currently sub-threshold for clinical use.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: We must initiate an immediate probe redesign for the MSH2 locus. Until that is validated, implement a mandatory Sanger sequencing backfill for this region for all Lynch syndrome referrals.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: Additionally, we need to optimize our wash stringency parameters to improve capture specificity and increase the on-target mapping rate to reach a 100x mean depth minimum.

script.speaker: Narrator
script.line: Let’s get these protocol adjustments into validation immediately to ensure we maintain clinical sensitivity. I will be in the dry lab if you need to review the coverage plots.
